_comment_2: Tyk Classic API definition for p11-resp-body. Canonical contract: gateway injects $.bench.injected=true and drops $.origin from the JSON response body before flushing it to the client.
_comment_3: Tyk OSS Classic ships exactly one native primitive that can do JSON-aware response-body mutation: `extended_paths.transform_response` with a Go template (text/template + Sprig v3). The shared template lives at ../_shared/templates/p11_response_rewrite.tmpl and is mounted into the container at /opt/tyk-gateway/middleware/bench-templates/p11_response_rewrite.tmpl by docker-compose.yaml. Sprig's set/unset/dict/mustToJson are exactly the four helpers needed for this contract — see the template file for the rationale.
_comment_4: JSVM `response` middleware in Tyk Classic OSS is gated on the gRPC/Python coprocess driver (see mw_coprocess_response_plugin.go in upstream); the otto-only `enable_jsvm: true` knob does not register a `response` array entry. `transform_jq_response` requires a custom Tyk build with the `jq` build tag — not present in tykio/tyk-gateway:v5.11.1. transform_response is the only canonical OSS-Classic path.
_comment_5: The fixture probes hit GET /anything (probes 1+2) and POST /anything (probe 3); we register one transform_response entry per method against the wildcard path `{rest:.*}` (gorilla/mux pattern). One entry per method is the minimum viable shape — adding HEAD/PUT/etc would cost nothing but the fixture never exercises them.
name: bench
